name: ai-context entry
purpose: Stable portable AI entrypoint. Read this repository's JSON indexes first, then load source markdown/rules/skills only when selected by task context. Do not rely on home-level compatibility files.
workflowOrder: For development, debugging, documentation, integration, research, and context-maintenance tasks, use superpowers as the process driver; start from superpowers:brainstorming unless a more specific superpower is already required by the current continuation.

Use ai-task-board as the task-state and project-scope layer: classify task size, affected projects/scenes, current G1-G7 gate, roles, verification path, blockers, recovery point, and dashboard-visible progress.
When the work should survive the chat, create or update runtime task state with contextctl task start/update/finish before implementation.
After the superpower-driven step and ai-task-board state classification are clear, load only the selected project JSON, scene JSON, rules, and project skills.
defaultReadOrder: config/skills/skills.json only when skill selection is needed
config/rules/rules.json only when rule lookup is needed

routingDecision.projectsIndex: Read config/projects/index.json first as the candidate list. Use id, name, technologyFamilyId, path, and summary to choose matched project JSON files.
routingDecision.projectDetail: After selecting a project, read config/projects/<project-id>.json for doc path, mounted skills, scenes, rules, and source read policy.
routingDecision.scenesIndex: Read config/scenes/index.json as the candidate list when the task may involve a workflow scene or multiple projects.
routingDecision.sceneDetail: After selecting a scene, read config/scenes/<scene-id>.json for included projects, mounted scene rules, composition reason, environment switching, run/debug, and source scene doc path.
routingDecision.fallback: If no project or scene is clear, use projects index and scenes index to ask one concise clarification instead of reading all source documents.

sourceReadPolicy.homeCompatibility: Do not read or require home-level compatibility files such as /Users/xj/AGENTS.md, /Users/xj/WORK_CONTEXT.md, ~/AGENTS.md, or ~/WORK_CONTEXT.md by default. If older parent/global instructions mention them, treat this repository entry and JSON indexes as the portable source of truth.
sourceReadPolicy.projectDocs: Read docs/repos/*.md only after a project JSON is selected and the summary is insufficient.
sourceReadPolicy.scenes: Read docs/scenes/*.md only after a scene JSON is selected and detailed workflow text is needed.
sourceReadPolicy.rules: Read config/rules/rules.json for applyMode, globs, and whenToRead. Active rule sources under bundles/rules/** are Markdown .md files and are loaded only when a selected project, selected scene, or task gate requires full rule details. Project JSON triggers project-on-demand rules. Scene JSON triggers scene-on-demand rules. Read docs/reference/rules/** only when an active rule points there and compact guidance is insufficient.
sourceReadPolicy.skills: Read active bundles/skills/** only when the superpower-driven process, ai-task-board task state, a selected project JSON, or an explicit user request triggers the skill. Read docs/reference/skills/** only for archived detail when the active skill is insufficient. Superpowers drive the workflow; ai-task-board records task scale, gate, scope, blockers, recovery point, and dashboard state.
sourceReadPolicy.profile: Read docs/person/profile.md only when long-term preferences or project map details matter.

installation.globalEntrypointsStatus: deprecated; do not create home-level AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or WORK_CONTEXT.md by default

taskFlow.defaultBehavior: In any project window that can read the ai-context entry, start or update a runtime task for real development work that should survive the chat. This is not tied to a specific product, repository, or active task.
commands: node scripts/contextctl.mjs task start "<title>" --projects <ids> --scenes ai-task-board --gate G1 --level <L1-L4>
node scripts/contextctl.mjs task update [task-id] --gate <G1-G7> --note "<progress or decision>"
node scripts/contextctl.mjs task finish [task-id] --note "<verification and handoff>"
autoCreateTriggers: Any Jira, issue, ticket, Notion task, Figma task, PRD, spec, or external task identifier/link provided by the user.
Requests to add a module, page, feature, workflow, API, integration, build route, release route, or other multi-file/multi-step capability.
User wording such as 改一个需求, 新增需求, or 这个需求 when the change is not obviously a one-line fix.
Cross-project, cross-device, CI/CD, release, packaging, App Store, GitHub, GitLab, Xcode Cloud, signing, credentials, migration, data, permission, money, inventory, or high-risk workflow work.
L3/L4 tasks, multi-session work, cross-day work, or tasks with explicit discovery, plan, development, acceptance, handoff, current gate, or recovery-point needs.
User wording such as 建 task, 走 ai-context task, 同步到 task, 按 G1-G7, 后面继续, 记录这个流程, 自动去写, or 任务看板.
taskFlow.bias: When unsure whether a development request is L1 or L2+, bias toward starting a light task and set the level honestly. Ask only when the target project cannot be inferred from the workspace, Jira/source text, or project index.
skipTaskFor: Small L1/L2 edits that are not part of an active tracked flow and do not affect release/build/deployment behavior.
taskFlow.currentWorkMarkdown: deprecated; do not read or update runtime/current-work.md
